Looking for Addiction Treatment in Southern California

Some people think Southern California is paradise. It is full of warm, sandy beaches, it rarely rains, and it never gets very cold. It is full of interesting attractions and tourist destinations like Malibu, Hollywood, Los Angeles, San Diego, Santa Barbara and Anaheim, home of Disneyland, the happiest place on earth.
Orange County is part of Southern California and encompasses resort cities like Santa Ana, Newport Beach, Irvine and Lake Forest.
With all the sun and fun going on, you may think the people in So-Cal have not a care in the world. But many of them are dealing with addiction.

It may be the ‘live fast die young’ spirit of Hollywood or the rejection that so many starlets deal with when they don’t make it to the big time. It could be the stress of living in a fast-paced city. It could be the hazy, lazy days that make people turn to recreational drugs until they become an issue.
But whatever the reason for addiction may be, it can get very serious and destroy lives.
If you are wondering how bad things are, here are some eye-opening statistics.
Drug Abuse in California
According to CDC.gov 15 of every 100,000 deaths that occur in Southern California are attributed to a drug overdose.

2011- 2012 data from the ObamaWhiteHouseArchives.gov reveals that California was among the states that reported the highest drug use in adolescents ages 12 – 17. 10.47% of the general population reported illegal drug use in the past month. 27% of drug treatment admissions were for marijuana while 35% were for stimulants. Drug-induced deaths were below the national average.
Drug Abuse in Lake Forest
Now let’s look at how Lake Forest compares.
According to 2014 statistics, 35.1% of the population engaged in binge drinking. Another report showed that 10,000 residents were hospitalized for drug-related causes in 2011- 2012.
Breakdowns on the drugs that led to hospitalization are as follows:
2016 data shows that 5% of hospitalizations were due to mental side effects of drug use.

Southern California hospitalizations and overdoses have gone up at an alarming rate due to opioid addiction. Overdose deaths rose by 88% from 2000 to 2015 and spiked even higher in 2017. Almost half of Orange County overdoses that occurred within this time period were due to drug or alcohol poisoning.
Achieve Lasting Recovery
What to Look For in a Drug Rehab in Lake Forest, CA
Drug addiction is a major issue in Southern California, but there are ways to get help. An inpatient rehab center may be the most effective solution.

A rehab facility will put you through detox to clean the toxins from your body. The staff will follow up with treatment that may include a variety of therapies that are best suited to your needs. You will be supervised 24/7 to ensure you get the support you need and don’t relapse.
After you complete rehab, the facility will follow up with aftercare to help you adjust to sober living.
If you are thinking of checking into a Lake Forest drug rehab, here are some things you should be looking for when finding the facility that’s right for you.

Skilled Staff: The right staff can make all the difference. The people at the facility should have a caring attitude and doctors should have a proven track record for getting successful results. After talking to the staff and doing a bit of research, you should be able to determine whether you will be in good hands.

The Right Therapies: A variety of therapies can be used to help clients overcome their addictions. Determine which therapies you prefer and find out if the drug rehab Lake Forest, CA you are thinking of checking into has those treatments available.

A Customized Approach: A Lake Forest drug rehab should understand that every client is different. As such, they should figure out a customized plan that works best for each individual. Ask plenty of questions to find out if the facility you’re considering takes a personalized approach.

Accommodations: Accommodations at rehab facilities run the gamut from basic to luxury. At the very least, you will want a drug rehab, Lake Forest, CA that offers clean accommodations and a soothing atmosphere. If you are looking for something more upscale, you will find centers with pools, spas, customized meal plans and more.
End to End Care: The facility you choose should offer end to end care offering everything from assisted detox to therapy to outpatient care.
Real Help for Substance Abuse
Hillside Mission’s Luxury Rehab
If you are looking for a Lake Forest drug rehab that offers a luxury atmosphere, affordable prices, and effective therapies, Hillside Mission is the ideal choice. Here is an example of what we offer.

Assisted Detox: After clients are checked in, they are immediately taken to detox. Detox can be the hardest part of a client’s journey as it causes unpleasant side effects that make many clients want to go back to using. Our detox takes place in a soothing atmosphere and clients are administered medications to ensure the process is as comfortable as possible. They are supervised to ensure relapse doesn’t happen.

Therapies: Hillside Mission realizes every person is different. We find out each individual’s needs and find the approach that is right for them. We aim to get to the root of addiction and provide healthy ways of coping that replace the urge to use. We base our therapies on finding a mind and body balance that will help clients achieve the ultimate health and happiness.

Aftercare: We understand how easy it is for clients to go back to using after they return to the ‘real world’. They may see the old crowd and the people they drank with. Their mouths may water when they pass the bar on the way home from work. Stress may come into play, and they may be tempted to take drugs or drink. We provide support and guidance that will help them adjust to sober living and maintain sobriety.

Luxury Atmosphere: We offer a soothing environment that includes a spa and pool and plenty of space to meditate. Our private rooms are clean and quiet. Our menu consists of delicious, chef-prepared meals.
Understanding What You Need
Get Help at Our Drug Rehab in Lake Forest

Overcoming addiction isn’t easy. Hillside Mission will make your journey go as smoothly as possible. Our comprehensive care will help you leave your demons behind so you can lead a happier, healthier life. Our caring staff will be there for you every step of the way. We will provide you with the tools you need to move forward.

Do not waste another day of your life. Call Hillside Mission’s drug rehab Lake Forest today. From there you can look forward to rebuilding relationships and getting on a path to lasting recovery and success.

managing-anxiety-and-panic-attacks.title: Effective Therapies for Managing Anxiety and Panic Attacks
managing-anxiety-and-panic-attacks.h1: Effective Therapies for Managing Anxiety and Panic Attacks
managing-anxiety-and-panic-attacks.description: Learn about the importance of managing anxiety and panic attacks and how Hillside Mission can help with dual diagnosis therapy.
managing-anxiety-and-panic-attacks.excerpt: Anxiety can strike at any time, with symptoms severe enough to disrupt daily function. Finding professional anxiety therapy can help you manage symptoms and prevent panic attacks from spiraling out of

blocks.text: Anxiety can strike at any time, with symptoms severe enough to disrupt daily function. Finding professional anxiety therapy can help you manage symptoms and prevent panic attacks from spiraling out of control if you are struggling with an anxiety disorder. Anxiety therapy does not have to be talk therapy alone; it can include things like medication, relaxation techniques, and exposure therapy.
Hillside Mission Recovery is a luxury drug rehab in Mission Viejo that can help.
Relaxation Techniques

If you or someone close to you is trying to manage anxiety and panic attacks, anxiety therapy is an excellent form of long-term treatment, but in the moment of an anxiety attack or severe anxiety level, relaxation techniques can help you prevent symptoms from getting worse.

Panic attacks are not something you need to learn to run away from or avoid. Relaxation techniques help you remain present until your anxiety has subsided, recognizing what you are experiencing and why it matters.
Surroundings
Several relaxation techniques might prove fruitful for you. The first is to take note of your surroundings. This is a form of mindfulness, and it helps you to take an actual inventory of things like:
This can help you focus on the present instead of trying to run away from your anxiety or letting it run away with your emotions.
Reassurances

Some people like to have some kind of reassurance, which might be a relaxation technique used in conjunction with your surroundings. With regular anxiety therapy, you might learn to practice things like rubbing the meat of your hand in between your thumb and forefinger to help you remain mindful. You might instead use calming and reassuring phrases like “I am having a panic attack. Nothing here can hurt me.”
Meditation

Meditation is another aspect of mindfulness that can help you in the midst of a panic attack or control other anxiety symptoms. Meditation takes so many forms it’s easy to find a meditative opportunity no matter where anxiety might strike.
For example:

items: People can utilize eating meditation during mealtime
Driving meditation while on the road in traffic or

blocks.text: No matter the type of meditation you use, it offers a chance to focus on your breathing, avoid clavicular breathing, and instead take deep breaths that calm the nervous system, slow down your blood flow and adrenaline, and keep your mind on the present moment.
Yoga

When anxiety is high, yoga can be a gentle and easy way to bring your focus to your breathing and the present moment while stretching out your body to physically alleviate tension. Holding basic yoga poses offers a chance to turn your attention inward, recognize that symptoms of anxiety or a panic attack are temporary and will always pass, and engage in what matters most.
Walking

Walking can be a great way to burn off adrenaline from anxiety, control fear, and overcome panic attack symptoms without breaking a sweat. Walking does not have to be extreme or fast to be beneficial. It is also something that can be done just about anywhere.
Walking is often more useful if it takes place in a quiet, natural setting, like a nearby park, a nature trail, or along a river.
Journaling

Again, the goal during anxiety symptom flare-ups or in the midst of a panic attack is to learn from it. You might need to know something important, like what recently triggered the attack or what things are making it worse.

Journaling during or after can provide an opportunity for introspection. Some people like to keep a journal where they can write down things they are experiencing amid a panic attack, while others might simply journal on their phones.
Exposure Therapy

In certain circumstances, more extreme forms of therapy are needed, like exposure therapy. Exposure therapy can be particularly beneficial in overcoming the triggers themselves. Treatment will take place gradually with the help of a professional therapist who gives you specific assignments.

items: If you have severe phobias regarding public spaces, your therapist might start by having you drive to a crowded Shopping Center and parking while remaining in your car for 15 minutes before going home.
The second task might be to drive to that shopping center with a friend and go inside one of the stores.
The third task might be going to one of the stores and having a coffee with your friend before driving home.

blocks.text: Medication Options

In certain cases, things like exposure therapy, traditional talk therapy, or relaxation techniques are insufficient. For these forms of severe anxiety, there are still opportunities to utilize anxiety therapy in conjunction with medication options.

Medication is most effective when combined with some form of therapy, like talk therapy, exposure therapy, and relaxation techniques. Non-benzodiazepine anti-anxiety medications or benzodiazepines can be effective forms of treatment for severe anxiety symptoms.

Depending on your circumstances, medication might be something you use short term, or it might be something that you rely on under specific circumstances for things like acute anxiety symptoms. In more severe cases, it might be something you use full-time to help you remain stable and avoid serious phobias or panic attacks.
If you are ready to get help, let Hillside Mission connect you to anxiety therapy that really works. Our Orange County inpatient treatment center can help.

blocks.text: Detox is the first step for many individuals beginning recovery from drug or alcohol addiction. It is the process of clearing substances from the body while managing withdrawal symptoms in a safe setting.

What Is Medical Detox
Medical detox is a structured process that helps individuals safely withdraw from substances under clinical supervision.
When the body becomes dependent on drugs or alcohol, stopping use can trigger withdrawal symptoms. These symptoms can vary based on the substance and level of use.
A medical detox program provides continuous monitoring and support to help manage these symptoms and keep individuals safe throughout the process.
At Hillside Mission, detox is designed to create a stable starting point for recovery while preparing clients for ongoing treatment.
Why Detox Should Be Medically Supervised
Withdrawal symptoms can range from mild discomfort to severe complications. Without medical support, some symptoms can escalate quickly.
Common withdrawal symptoms include:
Alcohol and benzodiazepines are especially known for producing dangerous withdrawal symptoms. Medical detox reduces these risks by providing immediate care and monitoring.
A supervised environment also helps individuals remain stable and supported during a challenging phase of recovery.
Substances That Often Require Detox
Certain substances are more likely to require medical detox due to the intensity of withdrawal symptoms.
These include:

items: Opioids such as heroin or prescription pain medication
Stimulants such as cocaine or methamphetamine

blocks.text: Each substance affects the body differently, which is why detox plans must be tailored to the individual.
What to Expect During Detox
Detox programs are designed to guide individuals through withdrawal in a structured and supportive way.
The process typically includes:
Initial Assessment
A full evaluation is completed to understand medical history and substance use patterns.
Stabilization
The body begins adjusting without substances. Staff monitor symptoms and provide support to maintain safety.
Medication Support
In some cases, medications are used to reduce discomfort and prevent complications.
Ongoing Monitoring
Clients are monitored throughout detox to ensure progress and stability.
This structured approach allows individuals to complete detox safely and transition into continued care.
Detox Is Only the First Step

Without continued care, individuals may struggle to maintain long term recovery. After detox, many people benefit from:

The Role of Mental Health in Detox

Substance use is often connected to these underlying issues. If mental health is not addressed, the risk of relapse increases.

medical-treatment-for-addiction.description: Contact Hillside Mission Recovery today to learn about the benefits for medical treatment for addiction and how we can help.
medical-treatment-for-addiction.excerpt: The fight against addiction has found an essential ally in medical treatment, guiding individuals toward recovery by amalgamating science, empathy, and optimism. The intricate nature of addiction, imp

blocks.text: The fight against addiction has found an essential ally in medical treatment, guiding individuals toward recovery by amalgamating science, empathy, and optimism. The intricate nature of addiction, impacting both the psyche and physiology, underscores the necessity of a comprehensive strategy for effective intervention. Hillside Mission Recovery recognizes the pivotal role of medical treatment in the journey to overcome addiction. This piece delves into the concept of medical treatment for addiction, spotlighting the transformative potential of medication-assisted strategies.

At Hillside Mission Detox, we have a thorough understanding of addiction and know how to assist you in permanently ending your drug and alcohol abuse. Every client who walks through our doors has a unique addiction, which is why we create individualized treatment plans for each and every one of them. Our competent and compassionate staff is committed to giving each and every client the highest quality care.
Contact us today to learn more about our luxury drug rehab in Mission Viejo.
Essential Elements of Medical Treatment for Addiction

Medical treatment for addiction entails a multidimensional approach that melds pharmacotherapy, medical supervision, and psychological bolstering. Departing from the conventional notion that addiction solely results from poor choices, modern comprehension underscores the profound impact of brain chemistry and genetics on fostering addictive behaviors. Medical treatment acknowledges this intricacy and strives to recalibrate brain equilibrium while aiding individuals in breaking free from the clutches of addiction.

Central to medical addiction treatment is Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT). This involves employing FDA-approved medications in conjunction with counseling and behavioral therapies to provide a comprehensive route to recovery. Notably effective for opioid and alcohol use disorders, MAT serves several purposes:

items: Craving Management: MAT medications help suppress the intense cravings, often culminating in relapse.
Withdrawal Symptom Mitigation: Detoxification’s physical and emotional challenges are alleviated by MAT medications, facilitating a smoother withdrawal process.
Euphoria Dampening: Specific medications can counteract the euphoric effects of drugs or alcohol, diminishing the incentive for their use.

blocks.text: Tailoring Treatment to Individual Needs

Hillside Mission Recovery underscores the uniqueness of each individual’s journey through addiction. Hence, medical treatment must be individualized to cater to precise requirements. Our seasoned medical experts meticulously evaluate each patient, discerning the most fitting medications and treatment plans. This personalized approach maximizes the prospects of a successful recovery.

A hurdle faced by medical addiction treatment is the associated stigma. Critics posit that using medications for addiction is merely substituting one substance for another—nonetheless, medical treatment frames addiction as a medical condition akin to any other chronic ailment. Just as people with diabetes need insulin to manage their health, individuals grappling with addiction can utilize medication to regain control of their lives.
Embracing Medical Treatment at Hillside Mission

In the battle against addiction, medical treatment, especially the MAT approach, emerges as a beacon of hope. By tending to both the physiological and psychological facets of addiction, medical intervention offers a holistic avenue to enhance the prospects of successful recovery. At Hillside Mission Recovery, we stand testament to the life-transforming potential of medical treatment, paving the way toward enduring sobriety.

Should you or someone you know contend with addiction, remember that seeking assistance exemplifies strength. Reach out to Hillside Mission Recovery today for a healing journey guided by our all-encompassing medical treatment approach at our Orange County substance abuse treatment center.

medications-used-in-rehab.title: How Are Medications Used in Rehab?
medications-used-in-rehab.h1: How Are Medications Used in Rehab?
medications-used-in-rehab.description: Medication can be beneficial in withdrawal and detox management. Learn how medications used in rehab can help your recovery journey.
medications-used-in-rehab.excerpt: Most of us know about rehab and how it is necessary for someone with an addiction to go through rehab to truly recover, but you may not know the role that medications play in the rehabilitation proces

blocks.text: Most of us know about rehab and how it is necessary for someone with an addiction to go through rehab to truly recover, but you may not know the role that medications play in the rehabilitation process. Rehab medication comes in a variety of forms and has a few different uses depending on the needs of the individual, but for many has become a vital part of the process of going through treatment, particularly when it comes to detox meds and their uses in getting people clean safely.

At Hillside Mission, we believe that everyone deserves to live a life free of addiction and that using medications is one of the ways to keep people safe while they go through the process of withdrawal and begin to get help. In this post, we are going to discuss medications used in rehab, including how they are used and which ones, as well as how to find comprehensive addiction treatment in Southern California.
How Are Medications Used in Rehab?

Although not everyone is aware, medication can be an important part of the treatment process. In fact, depending on the needs of the individual, there are a few different ways that medications can be used to either improve the success rate of treatment, manage medical issues related to the client, or deal with symptoms of withdrawal.

One of the more common uses for medication in rehab is managing the cravings that accompany withdrawal. Some medications can lessen the effects of other drugs and make it so that a person does not feel the constant urge to want to use again. This helps those who struggle with withdrawal or have relapsed in the past. Many times the cravings can get so intense that a person will do whatever they have to do to get more drugs to make the cravings go away.

Another use is in the management of symptoms of withdrawal. Besides controlling cravings, there are a wide range of symptoms that a person can experience when going through withdrawal from nausea and vomiting to full-blown seizures, depression, and more. Sometimes medication is used to treat or lessen the effect of these symptoms so that the person can complete the withdrawal process without any major incidents.

Lastly, medication may be used to manage existing health problems or to treat co-occurring mental disorders such as anxiety and depression. It is typically impossible to treat addiction without also dealing with the mental illness that a person also has, so in these cases, medication may be used as part of a treatment for these issues.
Which Medications Are Used During Treatment?

Depending on the situation, certain drugs are used as treatment during rehab. For example, some drugs are used to mimic the effect of other drugs and reduce cravings. These drugs, like Methadone, for instance, help reduce withdrawal symptoms while still helping a person detox from whatever drug they were addicted to.

Most of the time, this is used in severe cases where a person suffers from intense cravings and needs some relief. This can often make the difference between whether a person relapses or whether or not they complete the detox process and go on to continue treatment.

Some medications do not manage cravings, but instead, work to alleviate the physical and mental symptoms of withdrawal to make the process more tolerable. Additionally, some medications are used for specific drug or alcohol treatment to help deal with cravings and symptoms.

In other cases, some drugs like benzodiazepines and antidepressants are used to treat symptoms of anxiety and depression in those recovering from addiction. These can either be side effects of withdrawal or directly related to the addiction itself, which is known as a co-occurring disorder and means that the person has a mental illness tied to their addiction. Without treating both at the same time, it is impossible to ever truly recover from addiction because both conditions tend to feed off one another, and so long as one exists, the other will continue as well.

Lastly, medications are sometimes used to manage existing health issues while undergoing detox and treatment. The goal of medical supervision during the treatment process is to ensure that clients stay healthy while their body gets used to not being on drugs and alcohol. This can sometimes be made more difficult when a person has pre-existing health issues or develops health issues as a result of the addiction.
How to Find Comprehensive Addiction Treatment in Southern California

You could spend hours looking for facilities that offer medication assistance and other support, or you could come to Hillside Mission Recovery, where we take a truly comprehensive approach to addiction treatment, including detox medications and a wide range of therapy and treatment options.

Our Orange County residential inpatient facility is designed with luxury and comfort in mind so that clients can focus on their goal of getting sober. We provide access to aftercare services to continue the process once treatment ends. Contact Hillside Mission Recovery today if you or a loved one are suffering from addiction and need help. Our Mission Viejo rehab center is here to help through inpatient and clinical detox programs in Orange County.

Men Addiction Statistics

Data released after the most recent National Survey on Drug Use and Health indicates as many as 12% of American men meet the diagnostic criteria for a substance use disorder. This is compared to less than 7% of women meaning men experience substance use challenges at nearly twice the rate of females. Other statistics provided by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration and the National Survey on Drug Use and Health regarding men and addiction include:

items: Alcohol use disorders are more prevalent among men (12.4%) than women (4.9%)
More than 82 million males over age 21 reported abusing alcohol in the previous year
Approximately 26 million males over age 12 report misusing marijuana
Around 4.3 million youth and adult males report misusing opioids.
More than 33 million males report misusing prescription opioids

Research shows you will find more significant levels of success in an addiction treatment program where you feel comfortable. Data from several recent studies and surveys indicate that addressing addiction gender-specific elements and risks for addiction may improve the chances one will stay in treatment for the entire course of the program. Gender-focused treatment programs also improve treatment outcomes across both genders by breaking down many of the barriers and stigmas that hinder positive treatment outcomes.

What Are the Benefits of a Men's Rehab Program?

When people go to rehab, they each have unique needs. This also applies to the treatment needs between men and women. Gender-specific or men’s substance abuse treatment programs provide many benefits to help you heal and meet your treatment goals. For example:
The opportunity to share difficult experiences

Men in addiction treatment often have difficulty expressing difficult emotions or sharing their experiences related to addiction in a mixed-gender therapy environment. When participating in a co-ed treatment program, they may be reluctant to openly discuss the triggers and traumas that led to their addiction. A gender-specific treatment environment allows men to express and disclose worries and emotions in a rehab setting surrounded by peers who likely share a similar history.
Developing a new and like-minded peer support group

Men with substance use disorder often experience mood changes (including anger and problems with control) at a greater rate than women with substance use disorders. For this reason, men often experience more success in achieving and maintaining lasting sobriety in a gender-specific rehab where therapy addresses these issues while surrounded by other men who can relate to their emotions. Men with a history of aggression in emotional, physical, or sexual abuse situations should always choose a gender-specific addiction treatment program.
Gender-focused problem-solving tools

Women and men typically thrive in different therapeutic environments. Women are often more successful in treatment programs that are open and warmer. Conversely, men generally achieve greater levels of success in a therapeutic environment where sessions are less personal and more focused on problem-solving and behavior modification. Gender-specific rehabs like Hillside Mission consider these treatment considerations when designing their therapy programs.

blocks.text: Mission Viejo, Orange County
MHN Health Net

Acknowledging an unhealthy relationship with drugs or alcohol is only the first step toward beginning your journey to sobriety. The next step is to choose an addiction treatment program where you can safely detox and transition into a therapy program to learn more about the root causes of addiction and how to safely and effectively manage the stressors and challenges that lead you to drink or use drugs.

When researching the different types of addiction treatment programs, you have likely noticed that rehab can be costly. In fact, concerns about the cost of addiction treatment are one of the primary reasons many of the millions of people who could benefit from help at a program like ours at Hillside Mission choose to forgo professional recovery services and attempt to get sober independently. Unfortunately, this method of recovery, also called cold turkey, is often unsuccessful. In many cases, the withdrawal symptoms produced by detoxing from opioids, alcohol, and benzodiazepine drugs can quickly become overwhelming and too difficult to manage without help and support.

So how can you safely overcome addiction without creating any financial burden on your family? In 2010, the Affordable Care Act (ACA) changed the landscape of addiction and mental health treatment. The passing of the ACA required insurance companies to change how they covered addiction and mental health treatment as part of their insurance policies. Before these changes, insurance companies were able to restrict or entirely eliminate coverages for addiction treatment, especially in the case of relapse or necessary follow-up treatments after completing a primary treatment program. Today, private insurance, corporate-sponsored insurance, and federally funded programs all provide coverage for addiction and mental health treatment to varying levels.

What is MHN Health Net?

Managed Health Network, Inc. or MHN is a subsidiary of Health Net, LLC. The companies under the MHN umbrella include MHN Services, MHN Government Services, and Managed Health network. MHN offers behavioral health plans for medically necessary mental health and substance use disorder treatment. MHN behavioral health plans can be purchased as a separate plan or integrated with a more comprehensive Health Net insurance plan.
MHN Health net has several coverage levels that vary in coverage details. However, most offer similar essential services, including:
– Coverage for counseling and therapy sessions
– Inpatient, outpatient, and partial hospitalization program coverage
– Treatment follow-up, including aftercare services
– 24-hour crisis intervention
here is what you need to know
What is Health Net?

Health Net is a California-based insurance provider with more than three million members throughout the state of California. Health Net offers a range of health plans designed for people of all ages and medical needs, including individual and family plans as well as Medicare plans.
There are five basic Health Net plans to choose from, including:

items: CommunityCare HMO (Silver, Gold, and Platinum plans)
EnhancedCare PPO (Minimum, Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum plans)
PPO (Minimum, Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum plans)
PureCare One EPO (Minimum, Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum plans)

blocks.text: We are here for you
Does MHN Health Net Cover Substance Abuse Treatment?

In short, yes. However, as with many other insurance plans and providers, your level of coverage will vary depending on the type of plan you have. The passing of the Affordable Care Act in 2010 included mandatory coverage for substance use disorder treatment as one of the ten essential health benefits insurance plans are required to provide for their clients. This means that, regardless of the type of MHN plan you have, your plan will cover the same amount for treatment at a rehab facility like Hillside Mission as it would for a regular hospital visit.

Depending on your MHN plan, your insurance coverage may cover some or most of the inpatient (residential) and outpatient addiction treatment costs. Additionally, your plan may cover several alternate levels of care depending on your treatment needs. Examples of alternate levels of care include partial hospitalization and intensive outpatient programs.
do not hesitate to contact us.
What Types of Treatment are Covered by MHN Health Net?

The amount of coverage you have will vary depending on your length of stay in rehab, your insurance plan level, and whether the rehab is an in-network provider like Hillside Mission. Most MHN Health Net plans cover at least some of the costs associated with drug and alcohol addiction treatment. The type, intensity, and duration of the treatment you need to achieve sobriety will vary depending on several factors unique to your treatment needs. The most common types of treatments usually covered by MHN Healthnet include:
Detox

Sometimes, you may need to start your treatment by detoxing from alcohol and drugs. The detox process can be unpleasant, painful, and even dangerous or life-threatening. Medically-supported detox helps you safely withdraw from alcohol or drugs under careful medical and mental health supervision. Treatment providers may also administer medications to help you manage withdrawal symptoms.
Inpatient Programs

Inpatient or residential programs are live-in rehabs that provide drug and alcohol abuse treatment while you live onsite at the facility. Most inpatient programs last anywhere from 30–90 days, depending on your addiction treatment program and your individual needs and goals.
Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)

Medication-assisted treatment or MAT involves using medications along with addiction counseling and evidence-based behavioral therapies to improve treatment outcomes. MAT medications used as part of drug and alcohol use disorder treatment include Buprenorphine, Methadone, Naltrexone, and others.

Before beginning a drug or alcohol rehab program, it is essential to check your MHN Health Net policy to determine which costs associated with addiction treatment will be covered by your insurance policy. Knowing this information before beginning rehab can help you start your recovery journey with the peace of mind of knowing what out-of-pocket costs you should expect during treatment.

You can verify your coverage information by contacting your local MHN Health Net policy administrator or by logging into their website to learn more about what your policy covers. If you need help verifying your coverage, you can also call our admissions team at Hillside Mission. We have many years of experience working with insurance companies and can help you navigate the insurance verification process.

Newport Beach Drug Rehab
Overcoming Substance Abuse
Introduction

Addiction is all too common of a problem in America and all over the world. It ruins relationships, destroys families and it can even take lives. It is a disease that is hard to deal with and difficult to overcome.

Almost 21 million Americans struggle with addiction, yet only 10% of them get treatment. More than 700,000 people in the United States have died from an overdose between 1999 and 2017. Drug addiction has cost the U.S. economy over $600 million every year.

There are a variety of substances that cause addiction including alcohol, opioids, cocaine, crack methamphetamines, and more. Each one affects users differently but, unless sobriety is achieved and maintained, many of them lead to an unhappy end.

Addiction occurs in cities and towns all over the United States and Newport Beach has certainly seen its share. The peaceful beach community’s atmosphere makes recreational drugs appealing to some residents and sometimes things can get out of control.

Although the battle to overcome addiction is not an easy one to fight, there are ways to get help. Hillside Mission stands apart as a Newport Beach rehab facility that has seen promising results. Find out how we can help you leave addiction behind.

Learn the Statistics
Introduction to Addiction in Newport Beach

Newport Beach is a coastal community known for its busy harbor and its terrific waves. It is home to an old-time amusement park, several awesome beaches and cultural centers like the Orange County Museum of Art. It has a resort town vibe and pleasant temperatures year-round.

On the surface, Newport Beach seems like a picture-perfect town. And in many ways, it is. But it’s peaceful atmosphere also makes its many residents turn to recreational drugs to provide excitement and add to the relaxing environment of hazy lazy days on the beach.
Some people may experiment with drugs and keep used under control. For others, it becomes a problem that spirals out of hand quickly.

Drugs and alcohol can ruin relationships with family and friends. Users can lose their jobs. They can suffer financially. They may run into legal issues. They can engage in actions that hurt others and themselves. The possibility of an overdose is another matter that hangs over every addict’s shoulder.
No matter how bright and sunny it may look outside your window, there are always dark shadows when addiction is part of your life.
Achieve Lasting Recovery
Statistics of Addiction in Newport Beach
So exactly how bad is addiction in Newport Beach? Here are some eye-opening statistics.

Newport Beach is located in California’s Orange County. The county has seen a significant rise in drug cases throughout the years. Overdoses in the area rose 63% between 2005 and 2014. Between 2011 and 2016 over 1700 residents died from an overdose. 66% of these were caused by opioids.

According to the Coroner and Health Care Agency of Orange County, 782 residents paid visits to the emergency room between 2011 and 2015 for opioid related overdoses. 123 of these people could not be saved.
The county saw a 37% rise in fatal overdose emergency visits between 2011 and 2015. Fatal overdoses increased by 18%.
Most of these deaths were due to opioid use with a breakdown as follows:

Many overdoses occur when clients exceed prescribed doses. Approximately 74% of overdose deaths were caused by prescription opioids while 22% of users committed suicide. The cause of death was unknown for the remaining 4%.

blocks.text: Resources & Services
Orange County Behavioral Health
Residents in Orange County face many behavioral health disorders. Having access to local resources and services is key to helping individuals deal with these issues and get on the path to recovery.

Unfortunately, Orange County’s mental health faces a lot of challenges due to its location in proximity to the border, and the illegal drug trade. There are also issues with providing enough mental health services for the totality of the population. To improve the situation and get the people the help they need, it is necessary to examine the current extent of Orange County behavioral health disorders and ways to address them.
Understanding the Mental Health in Southern California
An Introduction to Mental Health in Orange County

Orange County’s mental health suffers from several gaps in available care coupled with a large population of people experiencing co-occurring disorders related to substance use disorder. This is due to several factors.
Breakdown of Mental Health in Orange County

According to the most recent statistics, Orange County is currently seeing a rise in the number of cases of mental illness developing as a result of drug and alcohol abuse, homelessness, adult and childhood trauma, and other environmental factors.

items: Mental health services needs among children stands at an estimated 5.9% of children aged 4-11
Of those children with mental health needs, an estimated 56.6% have gone untreated due to the gap in mental health services in orange county.
An estimated 6.7% of adults have serious mental health issues.
Of the 6.7% of adults with serious mental health issues, 46.6% have not received adequate care.

blocks.text: Among these statistics, the percentages of adults and children with serious mental health disorders can vary drastically based on factors such as income and education level, gender identity, homelessness, and patterns of abuse.

While the percentage of behavioral health disorders in areas like Santa Ana and surrounding communities is lower than the overall state average, the disparity in treatment as a result of inadequate access to mental health services has led to rising rates of behavioral health issues and lower treatment numbers.
Detoxification
Your first step in the right direction.
Detox offers a state-of-the-art drug & alcohol detox program that is safe & effective at our Mission Veijo facility.
Residential Inpatient
Two steps forward. No steps back.
The evidence-based approach at Hillside Mission’s luxury residential inpatient program in Mission Viejo works for you.
Aftercare & Alumni
Life on lifes terms.
We offer thorough aftercare planning & alumni programming to keep clients connected to the recovery community.
Know the Signs
Symptoms of Behavior or Mental Health Issues

Many different mental illnesses can manifest as a result of various factors, and understanding the symptoms of these illnesses can help to facilitate proper treatment, particularly in cases where individuals or family members may neglect treatment or be unable to determine the proper course of treatment.
Addiction

Addiction to drugs or alcohol also called substance use disorder, is defined as a chronic, relapsing disorder characterized by compulsive drug seeking, continued use despite harmful consequences, and long-lasting changes in the brain.

Initial exposure and severity of addiction can vary by substance. Addiction symptoms further manifest as a need for larger and larger quantities of the substance in order to satisfy the body’s need for the substance. This is also known as cravings, which can lead to further symptoms as a person becomes more dependent on the substance. This is commonly referred to as withdrawal.
The symptoms associated with the lack of an addicted substance can vary wildly from mild fatigue and discomfort to heart complications, seizures, and even death.
Depression
Depression, otherwise known as major depressive disorder, is a mood disorder that is most often characterized by broad sweeping changes in behavior as a result of the onset of the disorder.

Symptoms of depression usually begin with mood alteration including lack of energy, feelings of loss, emptiness, or hopelessness as well as sleep disturbances and inability to find pleasure in formerly pleasurable activities.

The range of symptoms can vary, and can even be quite severe and characterized by a general feeling of unhappiness. These symptoms can carry over to children, teens, and young adults and most often result in changes in day-to-day lifestyle activities as well.
Anxiety

Anxiety is a range of disorders whereby a person experiences persistent, chronic, and excessive fear, worry, or stress due to everyday events and situations. These feelings can onset quickly and can often overpower an individual’s ability to function in their day-to-day life.

Symptoms of anxiety disorders are characterized by an intense feeling of fear or danger from a typically ordinary situation. This can cause individuals to avoid doing things due to the sudden and often overabundant fear that is invoked due to an anxiety disorder. Symptoms can begin as early as childhood and continue into adulthood.

The onset of anxiety symptoms can be sudden and uncontrollable and can last for several hours. Some symptoms may be triggered by a particular situation such as social anxiety disorder. In this case, a person may experience extreme anxiety when around new people for the first time or when surrounded by large crowds.

Other anxiety disorders include generalized anxiety disorder, wherein normal day-to-day tasks can cause uncontrollable fear and panic. Certain anxiety disorders include specific phobias of places and things, and separation anxiety.
Some common symptoms include fear, sweating, tiredness, fatigue, and avoidance of people and situations that cause these issues.
PTSD

Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) is a mental health disorder brought about by extreme trauma. This disorder can cause individuals to relive traumatic experiences due to triggers that cause symptoms and reminders of the event. PTSD can occur at any age and can come about at any time after the initial incident.
Symptoms can include severe fatigue, sweating, nightmares, hallucinations, flashbacks, anxiety or the onset of anxiety-related disorders, and uncontrollable memories of the event.
Among the multitude of symptoms for PTSD, there are 4 primary categories that symptoms fall into:

items: Negative changes to thinking and mood
Physical and emotional changes, particularly in reaction to stress

blocks.text: Each of these categories can include a range of symptoms associated with the behavior. The severity of symptoms can also range from mild to severe, with severe symptoms causing disruption of day-to-day life, suicidal tendencies, and other life-threatening behavior.
ADD/ADHD

Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD) or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) is often diagnosed in early childhood, though it can be diagnosed in adults as well. These disorders can be difficult to identify and diagnose as symptoms are shared among many other disorders.

In order to diagnose ADD/ADHD, multiple symptoms must be present at one time which indicates the potential for the disorder. These symptoms can include general inattentiveness, inability to focus or concentrate, inability to follow through tasks to completion, as well as a number of other similar symptoms.

The second set of symptoms involve any signs of hyperactivity such as excessive movement or fidgeting, excessive talking, constant interruption to activities and behaviors, and more. Treatment options will change based on the presentation of the disorder.
Once a person has been diagnosed, they can have one of three presentations according to symptom severity:

items: Combination Presentation - Individual presents as both inattentive and hyperactive
Inattention Focused - Individual’s primary symptoms focus on inattentiveness
Hyperactivity Focused - Individual’s primary symptoms focus on hyperactivity

These services can include healthy living checklists, partnering with a healthcare agency to get medication or other treatment, support group services in the community or through an aftercare program, as well as child and family support.
Orange County Mental Health Services

Many behavioral health issues are often accompanied by mental health issues that also need to be addressed. Addiction in particular has a high rate of dual occurring disorders whereby a person experiences mental illness as a result of addiction or develops an addiction as a result of mental illness.

Mental health services in Orange County can include treatment from a psychiatrist or other counselor, medication to address the symptoms, emotional support, behavioral therapy, and other methods to address the underlying causes of the mental illness. In most cases, it is necessary to treat both behavioral and mental health issues concurrently in order to see effective results.

Introduction

Life is full of stressful situations, and everyone has different ways of dealing with them. Some people meditate, some punch a punching bag, others may turn to recreational drugs and alcohol to numb the pain.

Drugs and alcohol may be an occasional escape for some people, but for others, they can become addictive. When addiction sets in, it comes with its own set of problems. It can ruin relationships and it can lead to financial and legal issues. It can even be fatal.
Addiction is a disease that is all too common in America. Statistics show that 19.7 million individuals over 12 battled with a substance abuse disorder in 2017 alone.

People all over the world become addicted, even those that live in climates where the sun is always shining. Orange County, California is a region that’s known for its beaches and pleasant weather. But despite the beautiful, warm environment, there are many people who live there that have dark shadows that lead them to use drugs or alcohol.

Fortunately, there is help for people that become addicted. There are therapies available as well as inpatient and outpatient programs designed to make the transition to sober living easier to achieve.

Learn the Statistics
Addiction in Orange County

Orange County is a metropolitan area in California. It is known for its pleasant climate and beach communities. It encompasses cities like Santa Ana, Laguna Beach, Newport Beach, Irvine, Dana Point, Huntington Beach, and more. Anaheim, home of Disneyland, the happiest place on earth is also in Orange County.
To those who haven’t visited Orange County, it probably sounds like a dream. It has warm temperatures, ocean breezes, a resort vibe, and several terrific attractions. But it also has a dark side.

It may be that the beach atmosphere lulls people into a false sense of security. It may be that the hazy, lazy days make people grow restless, so they seek out other forms of entertainment. It may be peer pressure or the stresses of everyday life, but just like so many communities across America, Orange County has its share of individuals struggling with addiction.
Here are some eye-opening statistics:

items: According to the Orange County Health Care Agency, the number of fatal drug overdoses increased by 88% between 2000 and 2017.
Men in the OC are twice as likely to be hospitalized for drug use and twice as likely to die of an overdose as compared to women.
There is an average of 5000 substance abuse-related hospital stays each year and approximately 700 people die from drug-related causes.

The average patient that checks in for a drug-related issue stays in the hospital for 4.3 days resulting in medical bills that amount to $430 million overall. 76% of deaths are due to accidental overdoses. 51.5% are related to prescription drugs while 66.8% are caused by opioids.
Women are 1.6% more likely to overdose on prescription drugs as compared to men.

blocks.text: Here are some other factors to consider:

items: Alcohol Use: According to a 2012 study published by the Orange County Health Care Agency, men tended to drink more alcohol than women and most users were Caucasian. Those in the 45–64-year age group drank the most. People in higher income brackets drank more than lower-income people. 10% of drinkers admitted to driving while intoxicated.

Drug Use: The study also revealed that 34% of OC adults had used at least one illicit drug in their lifetime. 33% said they used marijuana regularly, 13% reported taking cocaine and 1% admitted to using heroin. 6% said they had misused prescription drugs at least once.

Hospitalization Rates: Unfortunately, drug-related hospitalization rates in Orange County have not improved much since 2011. 48% of patients were checked in for psychological symptoms due to drug and alcohol withdrawal. 24% of patients were diagnosed with drug dependence or chronic alcohol syndrome.

overcome-alcoholism-at-an-alcohol-rehab-.h1: Overcome Alcoholism at an Alcohol Rehab in Orange County
overcome-alcoholism-at-an-alcohol-rehab-.description: The only way to safely beat alcoholism is with professional help through alcohol rehab programs in recovery. Contact us today!
overcome-alcoholism-at-an-alcohol-rehab-.excerpt: Alcoholism is a chronic illness that can have devastating consequences, both for the person addicted to alcohol and their family. It is important to know what alcoholism looks like so that you know wh

blocks.text: Alcoholism is a chronic illness that can have devastating consequences, both for the person addicted to alcohol and their family. It is important to know what alcoholism looks like so that you know when a person may be suffering from alcoholism and how best to help them. Alcohol addiction can cause many problems in a person’s life – from physical health issues like liver disease or heart failure to mental health issues such as depression and anxiety.

The only way to safely beat alcoholism is with professional help through alcohol rehab programs in Orange County like Hillside Mission Recovery. In this post, we will explain alcoholism, its effects, and how getting help at an alcohol rehab in Orange County can make all the difference to the life of the person addicted and their family and friends.
What Is Alcoholism?

Alcoholism is a disease. It is most commonly called alcohol use disorder (AUD) by health professionals to identify the fact that it is a health-related issue and not something that should be handled out of a proper treatment facility.

Part of the reason that alcoholism is difficult to deal with without proper medical treatment has to do with the fact that the person addicted becomes dependent on the substance in order to manage daily life activities. Even mild alcohol use can lead to addiction
Common Signs of Alcoholism
Understanding the signs of alcoholism can help you get the help you or a loved one needs to start recovering from alcoholism.
Psychologists and other experts agree that an alcoholic is someone who exhibits at least two of the following symptoms:

items: Unsuccessful attempts to stop using alcohol
Spending a lot of time obtaining drinks or recovering from their effects
Interference with normal activities due to drinking habits like giving up social engagements
Participating in high-risk behaviors when under the influence
Continued use despite physical problems caused by excessive consumption

blocks.text: Withdrawal is one of the most severe side effects of alcohol addiction. Symptoms can include nausea, vomiting, and shaking. The key difference between withdrawal (which typically occurs outside of formal treatment settings) and going to an alcohol rehabilitation center is that one provides medical supervision while the other does not. Detoxing also helps with cravings during inpatient drug rehab.

It’s natural to be concerned when someone you know has a drinking problem or is addicted to alcohol. They may have changed their appearance by not showering, forgetting appointments, neglecting responsibilities at work or school.

When a person with alcoholism slips from sobriety back into addiction even for one day the probability of relapse increases dramatically because it becomes easier for them to justify continuing their unhealthy behavior in the future. They may justify drinking as a means to cope with daily life or due to other outside forces.

The most difficult part about being addicted to alcohol is that there are no quick fixes. Alcoholism is a chronic condition that needs to be treated as such and it cannot be dealt with alone without professional help or at least the support of others who have been through it themselves.

There are many resources available for those suffering from alcoholism, including an alcohol rehab in Orange County where people can go to get sober and start living life again on their own terms instead of letting addiction dictate how they live day-to-day.
What Happens When You Go to an Alcohol Rehab in Orange County?

An alcohol rehab in Orange County like ours here at Hillside Mission Recovery is a place where people can go to get sober. We offer many different services, including treatment for other mental health issues like anxiety and depression that often coincide with alcoholism. The first step toward recovery starts when you walk through the door of our rehab center. You will be given an evaluation to determine your specific needs for your individual case. No two alcoholism diagnoses are the same.

Treatment at our facility typically begins with detoxification in a safe medically supervised environment. Here we can manage the symptoms of withdrawal and ensure that our clients are well looked after throughout the process.
The next step is to move on to inpatient treatment, where clients will begin a treatment plan that is tailored to their unique case.
Once treatment ends we will help set up aftercare options such as peer support groups, 12 step programs, and other community services.

Hillside Mission Recovery is dedicated to getting our clients on the path to sobriety and helping them stay there with high-quality treatment options and care and compassion. If you or someone you know is suffering from alcoholism, contact us today!

overcoming-guilt-and-shame-in-addiction-.title: Overcoming Guilt and Shame in Addiction Recovery
overcoming-guilt-and-shame-in-addiction-.h1: Overcoming Guilt and Shame in Addiction Recovery
overcoming-guilt-and-shame-in-addiction-.description: Learn more about how to overcome guilt and shame to put yourself on a path to sustained sobriety. Speak to our admissions team to learn more about the therapy offered by our Mission Viejo luxury drug rehab center.
overcoming-guilt-and-shame-in-addiction-.excerpt: Navigating the challenges of addiction entails confronting a myriad of obstacles, and among them, the emotional rollercoaster stands out prominently. Throughout the journey to recovery, individuals gr

blocks.text: Navigating the challenges of addiction entails confronting a myriad of obstacles, and among them, the emotional rollercoaster stands out prominently. Throughout the journey to recovery, individuals grapple with a spectrum of emotions, often finding themselves overwhelmed by pervasive feelings of guilt and shame. Addiction, a complex and pervasive disorder impacting millions worldwide, not only manifests in physical ailments but also exacts a profound emotional and psychological toll. These negative emotions, formidable barriers to recovery, can ensnare individuals in a relentless cycle of addiction. However, a nuanced understanding of the nature of guilt and shame, coupled with effective strategies articulated by clinicians, provides a pathway to overcoming these powerful emotions and achieving sustained sobriety.

Hillside Mission Recovery is an Orange County inpatient treatment center that offers quality treatment for those in need. Speak to our admissions team to learn more about the therapy offered by our Mission Viejo luxury drug rehab center.
The Dynamics of Guilt and Shame in Addiction

Guilt and shame, potent and intertwined emotions, frequently accompany the landscape of addiction. Guilt, arising from the belief in committing wrongdoing with repercussions not only for oneself but also for those in one’s orbit, contrasts with shame, rooted in the sense of inherent flaw or unworthiness. Both emotions, often stigmatized by societal attitudes toward addiction, become intricate threads woven into the fabric of an individual’s struggle. Acknowledging and addressing these emotions through therapy becomes an essential step toward liberation.
Strategies for Overcoming Guilt and Shame in Addiction Recovery

Therapists employ diverse approaches to guide individuals through the intricate process of overcoming guilt and shame in addiction recovery. Fundamental to this journey is the cultivation of self-compassion and forgiveness. Recognizing addiction as a disease rather than a moral failing, individuals learn the crucial lesson of treating themselves with kindness and genuine forgiveness. Self-compassion serves as a transformative antidote to the detrimental self-blame that hampers recovery progress. Challenging negative self-talk becomes achievable through a foundation of self-compassion and understanding.

The tendency to isolate oneself when grappling with guilt and shame is a common reflex. However, overcoming these emotions necessitates a support network comprising friends, family, and support groups. Isolation, stemming from shame, works counterproductively in the long run. Seeking solace in shared experiences with others who understand these emotions becomes a pivotal step toward breaking free from the emotional rollercoaster.

Professional therapy and counseling, whether in one-on-one or group settings, offer tailored avenues for healing. Acknowledging the diversity of healing journeys, these sessions provide a safe space for individuals to explore their emotions and progress toward long-term sobriety. Connecting with peers who share similar experiences reinforces a sense of solidarity and diminishes the isolating effects of guilt and shame.

Making amends emerges as a proactive step in the journey to overcome guilt and shame. Taking responsibility for past actions involves personal apologies and, in some instances, concerted efforts to repair strained relationships. This process of acknowledging and rectifying past wrongs delves into the depths of emotions, allowing individuals to confront and ultimately surmount guilt and shame.

Mindfulness and meditation serve as powerful tools for those grappling with inner turmoil. By fostering self-awareness and redirecting attention to the present moment without judgment, meditation enables individuals to detach from feelings of guilt and shame. This practice empowers a refocus on the positive aspects of the recovery journey.
Embarking on Holistic Healing at Hillside Mission Recovery Center

At Hillside Mission Recovery Center, we blend evidence-based therapies with holistic practices in the serene backdrop of Mission Viejo. Our belief in integrating mind-body wellness underscores the limitless possibilities inherent in a sober and fulfilling life. Our dedicated team of experts stands ready to provide individualized support, guiding each client toward a sustained and rewarding lifestyle free from the shackles of addiction. We invite you to join us in Southern California, where comprehensive assistance awaits to facilitate your journey to overcoming addiction for good.

oxycontin-addiction-signs-to-look-for.title: Oxycontin Addiction Signs to Look Out For
oxycontin-addiction-signs-to-look-for.h1: Oxycontin Addiction Signs to Look Out For
oxycontin-addiction-signs-to-look-for.description: The Oxycontin addiction signs to look out for can include changes in mood, anxiety and or depression. If you or someone you know is showing signs of oxycontin addiction, contact us today.
oxycontin-addiction-signs-to-look-for.excerpt: There are many different kinds of drugs out there and it can be nearly impossible to know the signs and symptoms of addiction for each one. What’s truly distressing for many people is that addiction i

blocks.text: There are many different kinds of drugs out there and it can be nearly impossible to know the signs and symptoms of addiction for each one. What’s truly distressing for many people is that addiction is different for each individual so if you don’t know the common signs, you may accidentally overlook someone who is struggling with addiction and the opportunity to get them help before it is too late.

One of the most common drugs that many people become addicted to is oxycontin. At Hillside Mission Recovery, we want to provide you with information to understand addiction in all of its different forms so that you can identify if a loved one is struggling with addiction and get the right help. In this post we will examine Oxycontin, what it is, several common oxycontin addiction signs, and how to get help with oxycontin addiction today.
What Is Oxycontin?

Oxycontin is in a class of drugs known as opioids. Opioids are often prescribed as powerful pain relievers and as such, oxycontin is in widespread use among both the addicted and normal patients suffering from moderate to severe pain.

The specific drug oxycontin is a derivative form of the primary drug oxycodone. While oxycodone is the base form of the drug, oxycontin is actually much more prescribed because it is a time-released tablet form of the drug.

When abused, oxycontin tablets are often crushed and snorted, or dissolved into water and injected in order to feel an instantaneous effect of the drug in the system. This makes it an easy form of the drug to abuse.

The problem with Oxycontin, like other opioids, is that it attaches to the opioid receptors in the brain and makes the body dependent on them, which eventually leads to addiction. A person moves from taking the drug regularly to needing more to achieve the same effects, to dependency and ultimately complete addiction.
Oxycontin Addiction Signs

There are many oxycontin addiction signs that you can look out for to know whether or not a loved one is addicted to the drug. It is important to understand that the effects of addiction are different for each individual so it’s important to pay attention to any changes in behavior, especially if you know they have been prescribed oxycontin as a medication.

The first sign to look out for is if a person is taking the medication differently than it was prescribed. This can mean taking higher than the recommended dose, taking it more frequently, or changing the form in which they take the drug, such as snorting or injecting it.

Other signs include changes in mood, including anxiety and depression when coming off the drug. It is also common for people to become socially distant or to neglect life responsibilities in favor of taking the drug. They may even resort to illicit means to acquire the drug if they run out of their prescription, which can lead to them harming themselves or others in the process.
A person addicted will often continue using the drug no matter what type of harm the drug does to their life or body, which is why getting help is so important.
How to Get Help With an Oxycontin Addiction Today
If you notice the signs of Oxycontin addiction in someone you know, the first step to getting help is always to get the person to agree they need help.

The next step is getting help from a treatment facility like Hillside Mission Recovery. We offer custom-tailored inpatient rehab programs in a luxurious and calming environment that allows patients to recover in comfort and security without worrying about the stresses of daily life.

After treatment is complete clients can use our aftercare services to get ongoing help with returning to a normal life. If you or someone you know is showing signs of oxycontin addiction, contact Hillside Mission Recovery today.

paying-for-drug-rehab-what-are-my-option.title: Paying for Drug Rehab: What Are My Options?
paying-for-drug-rehab-what-are-my-option.h1: Paying for Drug Rehab: What Are My Options?
paying-for-drug-rehab-what-are-my-option.description: You might experience hindrances to your treatment, but a lack of finances will not be one of them. Learn about paying for rehab.
paying-for-drug-rehab-what-are-my-option.excerpt: Right after deciding to go to rehab and choosing a treatment center that suits you, the next thing you may be thinking of is how to pay for treatment. Paying for drug rehab is unfortunately a big deci

blocks.text: Right after deciding to go to rehab and choosing a treatment center that suits you, the next thing you may be thinking of is how to pay for treatment. Paying for drug rehab is unfortunately a big deciding factor for those looking to get sober. But please do not let that deter you from seeking treatment. It is an investment that will benefit you for the rest of your life.

You don’t have to be overly worried about figuring out how to afford rehab because everyone can. What you pay for drug rehab is dependent on the facility and the type of treatment options you go for. For instance, residential or inpatient treatment options cost more than partial hospitalization or outpatient treatment, but you can’t put a price tag on your life.
Even with all these factors, there is a rehab facility for every income bracket, and better still, there are great payment options available.
You might experience hindrances to your treatment, but a lack of finances will not be one of them. Keep reading to know how you can pay for rehab and what treatment options you can take advantage of.
How to Pay for Rehab
Although rehab is affordable for all, how much you’ll need to invest in your treatment would depend on what rehab services you need.
Also, like we mentioned above, paying for rehab is an investment. As you get better, you’ll find a job and save money, which would have previously been used to fund your addiction.

There are a few payment options that you could go with, but before you do, make a quick assessment of what you need. Find out what rehab services you need and how much they cost. Next, look at your options. Payment options for rehab include;
Use Insurance

Almost every health insurance, private or government-owned, covers drug rehab. When looking to utilize insurance, check what health issues your insurance covers, as sometimes drug rehab is under ‘behavioral coverage’. But to be sure, call your insurance company and find out exactly what your insurance covers.
Here is a list of things to ask about when you call your insurance company:

items: Will there be any co-payments? If yes; how much will it be?
Does my insurance cover medications, and if it does, what medications are covered?
How many outpatient sessions will my insurance cover?
How many days of inpatient care would my insurance cover?
Are the treatment options limited to some providers?

blocks.text: If you aren’t certain about how to pay for rehab with insurance, contact the treatment center and ask for guidance.
Pay Out of Pocket

If you aren’t eligible for insurance packages, paying out of pocket is another option for you. Paying out of pocket for drug rehab may expensive, but you can offset the burden by looking for financing.
Some rehab centers offer payment plans that are similar to college loans. With this option, you can incorporate the money into your monthly expenses and pay it off with time.

You can also apply for grants from the treatment center itself or organizations like SAMHSA. If you are paying out of pocket, contact the admissions office and discuss your options. These will vary from facility to facility.
Seek Financial Assistance
When all things fail, seek help. This could come in the form of loans from family and friends, crowdfunding, and even state assistance.
Don’t be afraid to take loans; paying for drug rehab might be tough but living with your addiction is far more difficult.
The battle for help isn’t only on your side; most treatment centers are dedicated to helping you find a way to fund your treatment.
Reach Out to Hillside Mission Today

At Hillside Mission treatment center, we work with most insurance companies to help individuals find their recovery path. We also offer a wide range of treatment services as well as a serene environment to help patients focus on their healing. Reach out to us today for help with your addiction!
